summary: Update the specification tests
description: Sync JSON specification test changes

summary: Convert GridFS spec tests to unified test format
Top level error labels aren't added to WriteConcernErrors
Stop skipping tests unnecessarily
description: There is a bug in the logic of {{ClusterFixture.serverVersionGreaterThan}}.  If you pass it a string like {{"5.0"}}. it will parse it and then create the ServerVersion instance with {{[5, 0, 0]}} and compare it against the actual server version.  But once the first 5.0 patch release is out, a test with {{@IgnoreIf(\{ serverVersionGreaterThan('5.0') }) }} will be incorrectly skipped when running against a 5.0.1 server. The intention is that the test is only skipped when running against the next minor release, e.g. 5.1.*.

summary: Update libmongocrypt bindings to use libmongocrypt 1.2.1
description:
*Motivation*
MONGOCRYPT-321 can cause a crash for libmongocrypt users with Azure or GCP as a KMS provider when oauth expires. This has been fixed in libmongocrypt [1.2.1|https://github.com/mongodb/libmongocrypt/releases/tag/1.2.1], but drivers that have separately versioned bindings will need to release on a commit at or after the 1.2.1 tag.

*Scope*
Drivers should update libmongocrypt bindings to a commit at or after the libmongocrypt 1.2.1 tag to receive the fix of MONGOCRYPT-321.

summary: Run Test Suites Against Serverless in Evergreen
description: Run a subset of specification tests against Serverless.

summary: Bump reactive streams dependency version from 1.0.2 to 1.0.3
description: Currently used reactive streams dependency has pretty old version 1.0.2 which dates back to 2017. Newer version of this dependency is avaliable (version 1.0.3) and is use by newest version of various reactive streams libraries like akka-streams or fs2-reactive-streams. So, adding this driver as a dependency creates restriction to use very old version of libraries. Please update version of this dependency up to new.

summary: DefaultConnectionPool and ConcurrentPool incorrectly handle some maxSize values
description:
* If {{maxSize}} is 0, then
** {{DefaultConnectionPool.createTimeoutException}} works incorrectly because it considers the value to be 0 instead of being infinite;
** {{ConcurrentPool}} is created with 0 semaphore permits and does not allow creating connections.
* If {{maxSize}} is negative, then
** {{ConcurrentPool}} creates a semaphore with negative permits, which means connections must be released before they can be created. The {{ConcurrentPool}} docs state _"if < 0 then no limit"_.

summary: Aggregation helper fails on simplified string form for $merge stage
Calling cursor.next() and cursor.close() concurrently in Load Balanced mode uses the same connection simultaneously
Slow tests not running as expected
description:
* *Junit4* 
  The 3 Junit4 tests using {{@Tag("Slow")}} are always run (as @Tag is a junit5 annotation)
* *Spock*
  The gradle configuration currently only runs "normal" tests for spock.
  Looks to using a single system property to determine which tests to include / exclude

summary: reactive-streams-tck-test doesn't run any tests
description: Not sure when this started but looking at [this recent task run|https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task/mongo_java_driver_reactive_streams_tck_test_reactive_streams_tck_test_2b621b3c761e2b145cada33f2d65beb58c9615f7_21_07_27_21_38_40], no tests appear to be running as part of the Gradle build task {{driver-reactive-streams:tckTest}}

summary: Not able to connect to primary node.
Ensure tests on new servers aren't relying on OP_KILL_CURSORS, OP_INSERT, OP_UPDATE, and OP_DELETE
Tests for client-side errors with snapshot reads use incorrect syntax for test.expectEvents
Split unified runner valid-fail tests with malformed operations into separate files
Integration tests for observeSensitiveCommands
description:
Driver Changes: Sync {{valid-pass}} tests for the unified test format to [mongodb/specifications@6847048|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/6847048c551431c8a328b9c1f0deeec158160d20]. Also note that some additional clarifications were added to the spec itself concerning implementation of {{observeSensitiveEvents}} (originally introduced in DRIVERS-1653); however, the behavior of the syntax has not been changed.  

----

{panel:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#21313c|titleBGColor=#21313c|titleColor=#fafbfc|bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*
DRIVERS-1653 introduced {{observeSensitiveCommands}} to the unified test runner (schema version 1.5); however, it did not include integration tests for the new syntax. The test cases pertaining to sensitive command redaction are not sufficient to verify that drivers have implemented the new syntax completely. This issue will add integration tests to do so.

h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?
Drivers.

h4. How does this affect the end user?
Does not affect the end user.

h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?
It's quite possible that {{observeSensitiveCommands}} can be implemented incorrectly if drivers are only using the redaction tests in DRIVERS-1653 to verify their implementations.

h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?
The risk is an incorrect test runner implementation.

h4. Is this issue urgent?
This should be done alongside DRIVERS-1653, but individual drivers can make that call.

h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?
No.

h4. Is this ticket only for tests?
Only tests.
{panel}

summary: Bump maxWireVersion in SDAM monitoring tests
description: Several of the SDAM monitoring tests include {{"maxWireVersion": 4}} in their {{hello}} responses. This will cause test failures once drivers bump their {{minWireVersion}} to 6 when implementing DRIVERS-1315. These tests should be updated to use a larger {{maxWireVersion}}.

summary: Deprecate mapReduce command
Test that KMS TLS connections verify peer certificates
Regex decoding error tests in top.json have unexpected, invalid syntax
description:
Downstream changes: drivers should sync their BSON corpus spec tests to [mongodb/specifications@5fad277|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/5fad2773960d63d46b7738dde8d7b1aca0ceaac9]. Two regex tests within {{top.json}}'s {{decodeErrors}} array have changed.

----

{panel:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#21313c|titleBGColor=#21313c|titleColor=#fafbfc|bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*
Two {{decodeErrors}} tests in {{top.json}} incorrectly use {{$options}} as a field within {{$regularExpression}}. That causes a parsing error, which can interfere with the actual error being expected by the test (using a number instead of a string for {{pattern}} or {{options}}). This error dates back to [mongodb/specifications@c6d7ac8|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/c6d7ac82cc48800171678b9515c92d69d5bebbcd#diff-1a68bac4dbe5dfffb2bda1086a4e0fa70114c7b56b26bf60a3ebb2d7508c2596R79].

h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?
Driver test suites may not be properly testing expected types for {{$regularExpression}} syntax in Extended JSON.

h4. Is this issue urgent?
No.

h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?
No.

h4. Is this ticket only for tests?
Yes.

{panel}

summary: Missing Import-Package entry in OSGI bundle
description:
You have a couple of imports in com.mongodb.internal.connection.SaslAuthenticator.

import javax.security.auth.Subject;
import javax.security.auth.login.LoginException;

These cause a ClassNotFound exception in OSGI/karaf environments

I suspect you need to add:

'javax.security.auth.*', to the afterEvaluate clause in the core build.gradle in the

jar.manifest.attributes['Import-Package'] section

summary: Speculative authentication always uses admin as the source
description:
Speculative authentication always uses "admin" as the source. 

In certain circumstances this can lead to a {{Attempt to switch database target during SASL authentication}} error when using a non admin auth source and duplicated usernames.

summary: BSON corpus code and symbol tests are partially testing the wrong types
description:
Downstream changes: drivers should sync their BSON corpus spec tests with [mongodb/specifications@c4cc034|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/c4cc034e290af27bb0ea554456d4f945a96d1736].

----

Originally reported here: https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/issues/1040

We should fix the incorrect code and symbol tests and audit the rest of bson corpus tests to make sure they're using the proper types too.

summary: InternalStreamConnection must close its stream if Stream.readAsync completes abruptly
description: Port the fix implemented in [dddab108d95d24ac6e083ab4f9c7049c27b1d314|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-java-driver/commit/dddab108d95d24ac6e083ab4f9c7049c27b1d314] to the {{master}} branch.

summary: Add BSON Binary subtype 7
Bump maxWireVersion to 14 for MongoDB 5.1
Test serialization of BSON with embedded null bytes in strings
description:
Downstream changes: drivers should sync BSON corpus spec tests with [mongodb/specifications@ff5d91f|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/ff5d91fea80b9dcb40fb2fb3533fd1bbcee818b0]. Specifically:

 * There are new {{decodeErrors}} tests for {{document.json}}, {{regex.json}}, and {{top.json}}
 * There are new {{parseErrors}} tests for {{top.json}}
 * There are new prose tests for encoding BSON documents and regex types.

Existing language in the spec was also relaxed to no longer _require_ drivers to successfully parse {{parseErrors}} tests in {{binary.json}} and {{top.json}} with a non-Extended JSON parser (to check for general JSON syntax errors). Also, an allowance was made for drivers that parse Extended JSON directly into language types (with regard to evaluating {{parseErrors}}).

----

Per DRIVERS-101, drivers are expected to validate that C strings do not contain embedded null bytes.

We should add spec or prose tests to verify BSON C strings with embedded null bytes are rejected when serialized.

summary: Disable isMaster-network-error, isMaster-command-error tests on servers before 4.9
CMAP integration test enables failpoint on isMaster which cannot be disabled
Fix race condition in the pool-checkout-no-idle test
CMAP integration test waits for wrong event
Fix race condition in pool-clear-min-size.yml
Fix race condition in pool-clear-clears-waitqueue.yml
Connection pools must be created and eventually marked ready for any server if a direct connection is used
Refactor read/write retries to accommodate for both PoolClearedError and CSOT

summary: Add standard Scala collection collect method to Observable
description:
The current collect method on Observable has a different signature than the standard [collect|https://www.scala-lang.org/api/2.12.1/scala/collection/Iterable.html#collect[B](pf:PartialFunction[A,B]):Iterable[B]] method on Scala collections. 

The suggestion here is to add the standard collections collect method on Observable, in addition to the non-standard collect already there.

summary: Propagate exceptions thrown in map or flatmap for Observable
CommandFailedEvent (and debug log) is generated if decoding fails
description:
Expected outcome:

For each command sent to the server, the driver should generate either a CommandSucceededEvent or a CommandFailedEvent.  

Actual outcome:

The driver generates both CommandSucceededEvent and CommandFailedEvent.

This can will happen in any situation where the registered Decoder for the response throws an exception decoding the BSON document response.

summary: Test Serverless behind a load balancer to prevent test breakage
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

The serverless test suite is currently run directly against Atlas proxies, as this allows drivers to run certain test against a single proxy if required (e.g. if a failpoint needs to be tripped) and target failpoints to individual proxies (e.g. for the mongos pinning tests). However, in the near future the proxies will be moving behind a load balancer, which makes both of these impossible.

The existing load balancer tests avoid this issue by spinning up two different load balancers, one that fronts a single mongos and one that fronts multiple, and conditionally uses each as necessary. We could consider doing something similar for serverless, though it would require changes Atlas side.

*Motivation*
 Without a solution to this problem, drivers testing of serverless will be limited.

_Does this ticket have a required timeline? What is it?_
 All serverless instances will be moving behind load balancers in ~1 month.
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

Yes
{panel}
*Notes for Language Authors*

Drivers that already implemented Serverless testing before load balancing must opt in to testing load balanced Serverless instances.
 - Pass {{LOADBALANCED=ON}} as an environment variable to {{.evergreen/serverless/create-instance.sh}} to create a load balanced AWS backed instance.
 - Use the new expansions {{SINGLE_ATLASPROXY_LB_URI}} and {{MULTI_ATLASPROXY_LB_URI}} in place of {{SINGLE_LB_MONGOS_URI}} and {{MULTI_LB_MONGOS_URI}} in load balancer tests.
 - See the [Go POC|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/pull/719/files] for an example of updating existing Serverless.

summary: Test that clients wait 500ms between failed isMaster checks
description: See DRIVERS-1251 for updated details.

summary: Evergreen: UnifiedCrudTest failures on latest
Make ConnectivityTest faster and more informative
description:
When one of the Atlas connectivity tests is failing, it's slow and annoying to figure out which of the Atlas test connection strings is actually failing.  This is because:

# We run the same test once for each URI, and the test results files just overwrite each other
# In order to make sure that all the tests run, we have to use {{--rerun-tasks}}, which makes the tests slow

We can fix both these problems by:

# Pass all the Atlas connection strings in to a single run of each ConnectivityTest, perhaps as a delimited list in a new system property, and
# Refactor each ConnectivityTest to be parameterized, and include the connection string (without credentials) as part of the test name, so that they are unique.

summary: Add a load balancer test case for SRV resolving to 0 hosts
description: This ticket was split from DRIVERS-1835, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Clarify usage / property name org.mongodb.test.transaction.uri
description:
Usage has evolved beyond the original transaction use case. 

The property name may no longer be correct or there may be the need for an alternative property to be added.

summary: Change stream cursors should be resilient to replicaset changes
